subject: english
title: Tiếng Anh Archimedes — Bài rà soát kiến thức (vào lớp 6 chuyên)
intro: Bài rà soát kiến thức Tiếng Anh chuẩn bị thi vào lớp 6 chuyên — Hệ thống giáo dục Archimedes. Gồm 6 phần: Ngữ âm, Từ vựng, Ngữ pháp, Giao tiếp, Đọc hiểu, Viết lại câu.
source: Trích bài rà soát kiến thức Archimedes (MS MY English Center — Cô Trà My)
_answerNote: Bộ ảnh KHÔNG có bảng đáp án chính thức (chỉ khoanh mẫu ở các câu E.g.). Toàn bộ 60 đáp án do Claude tự suy — đây là câu Tiếng Anh khách quan nên độ tin cậy cao, nhưng NÊN đối chiếu key chính thức. Lưu ý vài câu có thể tranh luận: Q44 (What's your city like?) chọn A 'It's green and clean' (D 'People are friendly...' cũng mô tả được); Q47 (T/F) diễn giải 'doesn't feel well' ≈ 'tired' → đánh giá True (hơi lỏng); các câu viết lại 56–60 chấm theo cụm điền, chấp nhận một số biến thể. Q23/Q36 stem có lỗi nhỏ trong bản gốc đã chỉnh cho hợp lý.

passages.title: Hoa's meals
passages.body: Hoa lives with her parents. She has three meals a day: breakfast, lunch and dinner. She usually has breakfast at home with her parents and her brother. She has bread, eggs and milk for breakfast. Sometimes, she likes to have some noodles for her breakfast. Her favorite food is rice and vegetables. When she's tired, she often drinks orange juice or apple juice. At school, she and her friends always have lunch at half past eleven. They often have fish, beef and vegetables for lunch. She has dinner with her parents at home at a quarter to seven. They often have chicken and soup for dinner. After dinner, they eat some bananas and grapes for dessert. Hoa likes having dinner because it is a big and happy meal of the day. Her family goes out for dinner twice a month.

passages.title: Clarence Birdseye and frozen food
passages.body:
In 1912, Clarence Birdseye went to live with the Inuit people of the North American Arctic. The Inuit taught him how to fish under the ice. As Birdseye pulled the fish from the water, he put them on the snow and continued fishing. The fish froze almost immediately because of the cold air. Birdseye went to eat the fish a few days later. He noticed that they tasted as fresh as the day he caught them. Usually, frozen fish tasted old, and less flavorful. He wondered why the fish tasted better. Maybe it was because of how fast they froze.
Birdseye moved to Massachusetts and began experimenting with freezing food. He developed a new way to freeze it. He blasted the food with air chilled to -43°C. After he developed this method, he founded the General Seafood Corporation. Soon, Birdseye began experimenting with freezing other types of food.
Frozen food was a success! It revolutionized the food industry. Now people could enjoy fresh tasting food at any time of the year. They could even eat food that was packaged far away. Clarence Birdseye has changed the way we eat.

Why did the fish freeze almost immediately after being pulled from the water?

options.text: Because the Arctic air is cold.
Because the fish like cold weather.
Because Birdseye put them on the ice.

questions.stem: How did the frozen fish taste when Birdseye ate them a few days later?

options.text: They tasted old and less flavorful.
They tasted as good as fresh fish.
They tasted more delicious than fresh fish.

questions.stem: How has Birdseye changed the way we eat?

options.text: We can enjoy fresh tasting food at any time of the year.
We can eat tastier Arctic fish.
We can eat fresh fish every day.
We can travel to the Arctic to eat fresh fish easily.

questions.stem: What does the word "experiment" mean?

options.text: to give something to somebody in exchange for money
to send food to another country
to change something good into something bad
to try a new way of doing something

questions.stem: What is the main idea of the story?

options.text: Inuit people live in the Arctic.

Rewrite the sentence using the words given. The sum is so difficult that we can't solve it.
